name: Samsung SmartThings API
what_it_does: Cloud REST API for controlling and automating Samsung SmartThings-compatible smart home devices including lights, thermostats, locks, sensors, and appliances across any brand supporting the platform.
use_cases: Querying device status for all smart home devices from agent-driven home automation
Executing device commands (turn on/off, set temperature, lock/unlock) via API
Listening to device events via webhook subscriptions for real-time automation
Managing scenes and automations programmatically from agent context
Integrating smart home state into broader agent decision-making workflows
not_for: Devices not compatible with SmartThings (no local Matter hub support for all protocols)
Industrial IoT requiring deterministic real-time control
best_when: An agent manages a mixed smart home environment where multiple brands are connected through SmartThings hub.
avoid_when: You want fully local processing without cloud dependency, or all devices are Matter-native and you can use direct Matter control.

auth.notes: Personal Access Tokens for script/agent access. OAuth2 for app-level access. Scopes control device type access and command permissions.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Developer API: free with Samsung account. Hardware costs apply for hubs and devices.
pricing.paid_tiers: No API pricing; SmartThings hub ~$100, devices vary
pricing.notes: API is free. Samsung cloud backend required. SmartThings transitioning to Matter support which may reduce some cloud dependency.

performance.rate_limits: 250 requests/15 minutes for most endpoints; documented in API docs

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: OpenAPI specification is available improving discoverability. Error responses are JSON with codes and messages. Rate limit headers included in responses.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Device commands are generally idempotent (turning on a light already on returns success). Subscription creation requires checking for existing subscriptions.
known_agent_gotchas: Rate limit of 250 req/15min is relatively low — agents doing bulk device polling will hit limits
Cloud dependency means command latency is 300ms+ even for local hub devices
Device capability names follow Samsung conventions — mapping to physical behavior requires docs
Webhook subscriptions require public HTTPS endpoint — local development needs ngrok or similar

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. OAuth2 with device-type scopes. Samsung cloud access required — data passes through Samsung servers. GDPR compliant for EU deployments.
